metadata.title: Asbestos — the foundational mass tort, the §524(g) bankruptcy-trust template, the asbestos↔talc convergence, and the contested double-dipping / EPA-ban fights
metadata.status: the trusts, §524(g), Johns-Manville, claim counts, and the EPA ban are FACT; the double-dipping question and the EPA-ban status are reported divergently — BOTH given with dates (per the present-conflicting-sources rule); dignified victim framing
metadata.frame: The ORIGIN of the mass-tort + liability-engineering lineage the corpus tracks. Johns-Manville's 1982 bankruptcy birthed the §524(g) trust system (1994) — the LEGITIMATE, court-supervised, consensual version of liability containment that the later ABUSES (J&J Texas two-step, DuPont→Chemours spinoff, Purdue/Sackler releases) descend from. Four strands: (1) §524(g) trusts (60+ active, $30-35B); (2) the asbestos↔TALC convergence (~40% of mesothelioma suits now allege talc — the J&J cross-link); (3) the CONTESTED double-dipping/trust-transparency debate (industry vs the GAO finding); (4) the EPA's 2024 chrysotile ban and its 2025 reconsideration (another regulate-then-reconsider episode). Ties to spec-jj-talc-texas-two-step (Liability_Engineering, Talc_Litigation), spec-opioid-crisis (SCOTUS), EPA, Camp Lejeune.

the_524g_template: Johns-Manville — the largest asbestos manufacturer — filed Chapter 11 in 1982 under the weight of claims; the resolution (the Manville Trust) became the precedent for the §524(g) amendment to the Bankruptcy Code (1994), which lets an asbestos debtor channel ALL current AND future claims into a court-confirmed TRUST (with a future-claimants' representative) and emerge clean. Today 60+ active §524(g) trusts hold ~$30-35B and pay mesothelioma claims. This is the LEGITIMATE, consensual, court-supervised template — the opposite end of the spectrum from the J&J Texas two-step (a solvent parent bankrupting a shell). grade: fact.
the_talc_convergence: The mass tort is migrating: in 2025 ~2,035 mesothelioma claims were filed (>99% settle; meso = ~48% of asbestos suits), and ~40% of mesothelioma suits now allege exposure via TALC (asbestos-contaminated talc) — directly linking the asbestos node to the J&J talc litigation. A Dec-2025 Maryland jury awarded $1.5B (Cherie Craft, peritoneal mesothelioma from J&J talc) — one of the largest talc verdicts, illustrating why J&J's bankruptcy-containment attempts (which failed on the dated record) mattered so much to it. grade: fact.
the_double_dipping_debate: CONTESTED — both sides, with dates: (A) INDUSTRY/TORT-REFORM view (US Chamber ILR, ATRA, ~2014-onward): the two-tier system (sue solvent defendants in court AND file with bankruptcy trusts) enables 'double-dipping'/fraud — plaintiffs allegedly tailor exposure histories differently for court vs trust; 17 states (since Georgia 2007) passed trust-transparency laws, and the federal FACT Act was repeatedly proposed. (B) GAO / PLAINTIFF-BAR view: a GAO study identified NO fraudulent claim, and victims' advocates (CenterJD et al.) argue multi-trust recovery is LEGITIMATE because victims were genuinely exposed to many companies' products; the FACT Act would delay/deny dying claimants. WEIGHT: 'double-dipping is rampant fraud' is an assertion the GAO did not substantiate; that multi-source recovery occurs is undisputed — what's disputed is whether it's fraud or legitimate. Both retained. grade: the transparency laws + the GAO finding FACT; 'fraud' framing contested/unsubstantiated.
the_epa_ban: Also reported divergently, with dates: (A) BAN (Mar 2024): EPA finalized a ban on ongoing uses of chrysotile asbestos (the only form still used/imported in the US) under TSCA — hailed as historic. (B) RECONSIDERATION (2025): the US Fifth Circuit granted an ABEYANCE giving EPA ~6 months to RECONSIDER the rule amid legal challenges, and EPA signaled it may revisit/weaken it (ADAO 'alarm'). So as of the dated record the ban is FINALIZED-BUT-UNDER-RECONSIDERATION, not settled — the same regulate-then-reconsider pattern as the PFAS rollback. grade: the 2024 final rule + the 2025 abeyance/reconsideration both FACT.
the_honest_reading: Asbestos is the template case: the §524(g) trust is the LEGITIMATE, court-supervised way to compensate current+future victims from an insolvent defendant — and it is precisely the model the later ABUSES (Texas two-step, liability spinoffs, owner-shielding releases) try to mimic WITHOUT the insolvency or the court's consent. The harm is real and ongoing (meso still kills; the tort is converging on talc). Two genuine disputes remain unsettled and are presented both-ways-with-dates: whether multi-trust recovery is fraud (industry) or legitimate (GAO/plaintiffs), and whether the 2024 chrysotile ban survives reconsideration. Dignified framing for the exposed. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.

sources.name: Lanier — Johns Manville asbestos trust (the §524(g) precedent)
Worthington Caron / KCIC 2025 — talc-related mesothelioma claims rising
EPA — finalizes ban on ongoing uses of asbestos (Mar 2024)
Ogletree — EPA 2024 asbestos ban paused; Fifth Circuit abeyance for reconsideration (2025)
CenterJD — group letter opposing the FACT Act (plaintiff/GAO view)
